mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and residential housing starts signals broad-based economic expansion, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative directional accuracy below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising core consumer prices force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which increases discount rates and suppresses equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL accelerates above the threshold during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in housing starts reflects rising household confidence and credit availability, which transmits to broader corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields a negative predictive value below 45% across all market conditions, or housing starts decline while SPX advances consistently.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 150,000 additions for two consecutive months, labor demand outpaces supply, validating corporate revenue growth expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150000 in two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered episodes, or payroll growth decouples from equity performance during the observation window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with stagnant industrial output indicate demand driven by debt rather than income growth, which increases default risk and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 50% of historical instances, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During supply-constrained regimes, a sharp decline in the industrial production index alongside rising consumer prices triggers stagflation fears, which compress equity multiples and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or statistically neutral when industrial production contracts sharply alongside rising inflation, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.
